title: Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum show what’s possible in Celtics’ win over Warriors: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jaylen Brown scored 32 points and Jayson Tatum added 24 in the Celtics' 120-99 win over the Warriors.
Brown passed Jo Jo White and Dave Cowens on the Celtics’ all-time scoring list, reaching 10th place.
Kristaps Porzingis, now with the Warriors, received a tribute video for his contributions to the Celtics' 2024 title.

title: Steve Kerr explains how the Celtics went from an ‘afterthought’ to a ‘real favorite’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Steve Kerr praises Boston Celtics' development, highlighting their rise as a favorite despite Jayson Tatum's early season absence.
The Celtics, now second in the Eastern Conference, swept the season series against the Warriors with a 120-99 win.
Kerr credits the Celtics' success to strong player development and tactical improvements, emphasizing their team spacing and execution.

title: Advocates sue Worcester over response to mental health 911 calls, alleging discrimination
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Advocates filed a federal lawsuit against Worcester for sending armed police to all mental health 911 calls, alleging discrimination.
The lawsuit notes incidents where Worcester police escalated interactions with individuals experiencing mental health crises, causing harm.
Worcester's pilot Mental Health Crisis Response Team disbanded after 19 months; less than 25% of police trained in crisis intervention.

title: Hilary Duff will be Northeastern University’s 2026 commencement speaker
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Hilary Duff announced as Northeastern University's 2026 commencement speaker via Y2K-themed social media post.
Northeastern's 2026 commencement on April 29 will be held in Fenway Park.
Duff, known for 'Lizzy Maguire,' recently released an album after ten years.

title: 3 roster needs Patriots must still address after free-agency spending spree
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots need a proven No. 1 wide receiver, with A.J. Brown being a potential target.
New England seeks to enhance its pass rush, possibly targeting the draft for an explosive edge rusher.
Depth is needed at offensive tackle due to rookie struggles and the age of starting right tackle Morgan Moses.

title: Needham 16-year-old charged with murder in Roxbury slaying of woman, 61
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A 16-year-old from Needham is charged with murder for the fatal shooting of Marie Molea-Anthony in Roxbury.
The teenager, already in custody on unrelated charges, faces additional counts of aggravated assault and firearm possession.
Boston police are investigating the October 13 shooting and urge the public to share any information via their tip line.

title: Chelsea man released decades after murder conviction won’t be retried, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Thomas Rosa Jr., 64, will not face a retrial for a 1985 murder after spending 34 years in prison.
Suffolk County District Attorney's Office dropped charges due to new DNA evidence and lost evidence.
Rosa was released in 2020 and is the 100th exoneree from Massachusetts since 1989.

title: Former Attleboro man gets 35-40 years in prison for raping 5-year-old niece
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: William Machado, 35, was sentenced to 35-40 years in prison for raping a 5-year-old niece, followed by 10 years probation.
Machado was convicted on charges including rape of a child, indecent assault and battery, and drugging for sexual intercourse.
He fled to Chicago post-incident, but was arrested with U.S. Marshals' help and extradited back to Massachusetts for trial.

title: A month after tragic shooting, Colin Dorgan, Blackstone Valley hockey triumph to win R.I. state title
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Blackstone Valley's hockey team won the Rhode Island Division II state title, defeating Lincoln High School 3-2 in quadruple overtime.
Senior Colin Dorgan scored the tying goal in regulation; he lost family members in a shooting during a prior game.
The team wore jerseys with initials of Dorgan's deceased relatives during their tournament run, symbolizing community support.

title: Bruins call up former 1st-round pick Lukas Reichel on emergency basis
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins recalled Lukas Reichel from Providence on an emergency basis ahead of their game against the Winnipeg Jets.
Reichel, a trade acquisition from Vancouver for a 2026 sixth-round pick, has five points in three AHL games this month.
In his NHL career, Reichel has 22 goals and 59 points across 88 games, having been traded twice this season.
